title: psorospermum corymbiferum var. corymbiferum
intro: Psorospermum corymbiferum var. corymbiferum, also known as the quinine tree, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Hypericaceae. The plant's bark and leaves contain compounds with medicinal properties, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Psorospermum corymbiferum var. corymbiferum include its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antipyretic properties, making it a potential treatment for fever, malaria, and other diseases. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, to exploit its medicinal properties.
